stem: If a new graph of three linear equations is created using the system of equations shown and the equation x + 4y = −16, how many solutions (x, y) will the resulting system of three equations have?
explanation: The two lines already shown meet only at (8, 2), so any common solution of all three must be (8, 2). But (8, 2) does not satisfy x + 4y = −16 (8 + 8 = 16 ≠ −16), so the three-equation system has zero solutions.
